stage: S6 + S6b + S6d deployed and verified in production. S1a VERIFY awaits only the three-day read-back (~2026-07-26).
deployed: S5 yes. S6 yes — deployed 2026-07-22, migration 006 applied in production, and all four write/consume paths exercised against real services. S6b (read API + owner screen) deployed 2026-07-22.
gate: S1a IMPL owner-confirmed; VERIFY 5 of 6 steps run and passed by the owner 2026-07-23 (launch 201, edit 409, stop-bare 422, budget 201, stop 201, story reads back). Only step 3 — the three-day read-back (~2026-07-26) — remains; the gate and M1 close there, not before. S5 complete and live. S6 DOC+CONTRACT+IMPL+VERIFY complete. S6b DOC+CONTRACT+IMPL+VERIFY complete.
s1a_verify: Run against exp-001/v1 through the pod 2026-07-23. exp-001/v1 now holds a VERIFICATION chain (launch+budget_change+stop), NOT the real first experiment: its stop reason is a test and its launch text carries a stray line break from a multi-line shell paste. Owner decision 2026-07-23: leave v1 as the verify record; the REAL first experiment launches under EXPERIMENT_VERSION=v2. Incident, resolved: edit (step 2) ran before launch (step 1), so the sentinel 'EDITED...' text became the canonical launch under the deterministic id and the real launch hit 409; the one poisoned row was removed via the documented trigger-bypass (DISABLE/ENABLE decision_artefact_immutable inside the delete txn, as 2026-07-22) and the trigger re-verified enabled (pg_trigger.tgenabled='O'). Two follow-ups filed in TASKS.md: (1) s1a-verify.sh defaults to the real exp-001 and its edit probe can create the launch if run first — must default to a throwaway id and refuse to be the first write; (2) budget accepted newBudgetCap '1100' without the 2dp previousBudgetCap carries.
layout: one repository, several containers: services/core -> growth-core:3376 (ClusterIP only); services/web -> growth-web:3377 (bazos.alfares.cz/l)

growth-web.ingress: bazos.alfares.cz/l — a separate Ingress object for that host, NOT growth.alfares.cz. The gsid cookie is scoped Domain=bazos.alfares.cz so bazos-service can read it; another host leaves attribution permanently empty while reporting healthy (D-005).

database.name: growth_core
database.note: Roles split 2026-07-20. The runtime role holds DML grants only and owns nothing. Migrations 001-006 applied in production (006 confirmed applied via the migrate init container 2026-07-22).

schemas.qualification: S6, migration 006, APPLIED IN PRODUCTION. lead (anchor, from growth.lead-created) + lead_qualification (append-only). Append-only VERIFIED in production 2026-07-22: the runtime role holds only SELECT,INSERT and a direct UPDATE and DELETE were both refused with 'permission denied for table lead_qualification'. lead_qualification has NO foreign key to lead on purpose.
schemas.spend: S6, migration 006, APPLIED IN PRODUCTION. manual_observation, owner-typed ad spend. amount_value NUMERIC(20,4), rendered as a decimal string everywhere. superseded_by_observation_id exists for S8 and is unused.

leads_database.note: LeadQualification table added by prisma/migrations/20260722_add_lead_qualification and APPLIED in production (prisma migrate status: 'Database schema is up to date', 7 migrations, 2026-07-22).

exchanges.growth.events: topic, durable, routing key = event type. Produced by growth-core ingest drain, carrying growth.auth_redirect.initiated.v1 from bazos-service. Consumed by growth-core into queue growth.auth-redirects, which the consumer declares and binds itself on boot.
exchanges.auth.events: topic, durable. Produced by auth-microservice (W3). Consumed by growth-core into queue growth.auth-registrations.
exchanges.leads.events: topic, durable. Produced by leads-microservice. Carries growth.lead.created_from_registration.v1 (S5 W5) and, once S6 ships, growth.lead.qualification_recorded.v1. growth-core consumes both into growth.lead-created and growth.lead-qualification, which QualificationConsumer declares AND binds itself on boot.

broker.unconsumed_backlog_warning: RESOLVED. growth.lead-created, growth.lead-qualification, growth.auth-redirects and growth.auth-registrations all had 1 consumer and 0 messages on 2026-07-22 after the S6 deploy. No backlog was stranded.
broker.note: A topic exchange discards a message with no matching binding, so the consumer declares AND binds both queues on boot rather than relying on them existing. No dead-letter queue yet: unparseable messages are dropped with their body logged, failed joins are requeued indefinitely.

s6.built: LeadQualificationEvent (growth.lead.qualification_recorded.v1, contract C-006), the manual marking surface (two buttons in the leads admin panel that already existed, POST /api/admin/leads/:id/qualification behind AdminAuthGuard), ManualSpendObservation intake (POST /spend/observations on growth-core), and growth-core consuming both growth.lead-created and growth.lead-qualification.
s6.notBuilt: Nothing from F-006 §3 remains unbuilt — the experiment view shipped as S6b. See s6b below.
s6.contractDefectsFixed: F-006 claimed a PATCH /leads/:id status endpoint that does not exist — qualification is a new surface, not a decoration on an existing one. spend.observed_manual.v1.json accepted a blank evidenceReference (no minLength), which would have allowed a hand-typed spend figure with no provenance.
s6.pendingIsDerived: pending is never stored and is not a valid status anywhere — it is the absence of a judgement. Pending leads stay in the numerator of cost-per-qualified, and a derived pending cannot drift from that rule.

verifiedInProduction.leadReachedQualificationLead: PROVEN end to end from the real landing: bazos.alfares.cz/l/v1-cena -> consent (gsid cookie, Domain=bazos.alfares.cz) -> /l/intent -> /ui/auth-redirect -> auth register with state -> attribution.auth_redirect + registration + identity_link joined on correlationId -> qualification.lead. leadId d5efeb20-a5da-44ba-ad08-acdacaa25c08.
verifiedInProduction.judgementFromAdminPanel: PROVEN. POST https://leads.alfares.cz/api/admin/leads/<id>/qualification -> 201 -> qualification.lead_qualification. decided_by_id is the real auth user id (e2a6fdd4-...), NOT the string 'owner', as C-006 §1.3 requires.
verifiedInProduction.correctionAppends: PROVEN. A second POST with supersedesQualificationId produced a SECOND row; the original was untouched. Then falsified: UPDATE and DELETE as the runtime role were both refused, permission denied.
verifiedInProduction.spendObservation: PROVEN. POST /spend/observations -> 201, row stored with amount_value 1500.0000 exactly, is_manual true, and the ingest buffer row reached status 'published' (confirm-channel, so the broker durably has it).

s6.ownerDecisionResolved: Spend campaign dimension: DECIDED 2026-07-23 and built in S6d as growth.spend.observed_manual.v2 with an optional campaignId (C-006 2.5). Was: 'Spend has no campaign dimension...'

notes: growth-core 280 tests (243 before S6b, 276 after, +4 for the supersession chain; 24 suites, no baseline test lost), leads-microservice 174, growth-web 33. Landing copy read and APPROVED by a native Czech speaker 2026-07-23, before any paid traffic. Verified in production 2026-07-22: the full S6 chain and the S6b report. TEST DATA REMOVED 2026-07-22 on owner instruction — growth_core is empty (every table 0 rows), and the leads/auth artefacts of the verification run are gone. exp-001 no longer carries the 1750.5000 CZK it never spent. Deleting governance.decision_artefact required disabling its immutability trigger inside the deleting transaction; the trigger was re-enabled and then re-proven to refuse a DELETE. The 610 accounts matching %@example.invalid predate S6, belong to other smoke tests, and were deliberately NOT touched. See TASKS.md.

s6c.status: PLANNED, blocked by S1b. No code exists. Added to the delivery plan 2026-07-23 on owner decision.
s6c.what: The owner's cabinet — decisions recorded from a browser instead of scripts/s1a-verify.sh: hypothesis, budget, reason for a budget change, reason for a stop, spend entry, report, and the decision history of one experiment read back as a story.
s6c.why: Owner requirement: a decision record that costs a hand-assembled JSON in bash loses to two minutes in the Google Ads UI, and then decision_artefact is empty and nothing knows why the money was spent.
s6c.blockedBy: S1b, whose scope was extended on 2026-07-23 to include the owner's browser login (POST /auth/login -> session cookie -> POST /auth/validate against auth-microservice). Grants authorise API calls; they do not log a human in, so without that addition S6c would have been waiting for something S1b never produced.
s6c.milestone: M3, because S1b is M3. Until then decisions are written with scripts/s1a-verify.sh.
s6c.buildsOn: No new table and no migration. DecisionService (S1a), SpendService (S6) and ExperimentReportService (S6b) already carry every write and every number.
s6c.notInScope: Google Ads itself. No connector exists (S8 read, S9 write, both planned), so the cabinet records decisions and never changes a live campaign.

attribution.joinKey: payload.correlationId - never the envelope's, which auth mints for every registration including direct signups
attribution.gsid: verified with a constant-time HMAC check; only the proven sessionId is stored, never the token. Forged tokens cost the attribution, not the conversion.
attribution.notImplemented: gsid_orphan - W2 now produces touchpoints, so this is implementable; see TASKS.md

s6b.built: The experiment report. GET /experiments/:id/report (JSON read API) and GET /experiments/:id (server-rendered owner screen with a spend entry form posting to POST /experiments/:id/spend). Derives cost per registration, cost per qualified lead, the attributed/unattributed split and a derived pending. Owns no table and adds no migration.
s6b.money: Summed as BigInt scaled 10^4 parsed from the NUMERIC(20,4) text — never through Number/parseFloat. Cost metrics round half-up to 2dp once, at the end, and are returned as strings.
s6b.divisionSafety: Zero registrations or zero qualified -> null in the API, '—' on screen. Never 0, never NaN, never Infinity. Verified in production: the live report returned costPerQualifiedLead null with 0 qualified.
s6b.mixedCurrency: More than one currency in an experiment's observations -> no total and no cost metrics, with the reason stated on screen. Summing CZK and EUR would be wrong and look fine.
s6b.exposure: growth-core ONLY, which has no ingress. NOT on growth-web: growth-web is public on bazos.alfares.cz/l with no authentication, so an owner-only screen cannot live there. Owner reaches it via kubectl port-forward deploy/growth-core 3376:3376.
s6b.ownerDecisionTaken: 2026-07-23: the screen is published only behind a real login through auth-microservice, as part of S6c, gated on S1b. HTTP Basic behind an ingress was offered and refused. Until then growth-core has no ingress and port-forward is the access control. C-006 §6.8.
s6b.scopeGap: CLOSED by S6d, 2026-07-23. The report no longer counts every workspace lead against the named experiment: a lead's experiment is derived from its touchpoint, and leads it must not count are reported as outOfScope rather than dropped. C-006 6.6.
s6b.readModelFix: 2026-07-23, commit 181529b, NOT YET DEPLOYED. currentVerdicts() ranked a lead's judgements by decided_at and never read supersedes_qualification_id, so a redelivered or late correction, a decided_at tie, or a first judgement re-emitted after a correction chain all surfaced a superseded verdict as current and fed costPerQualifiedLead silently. Current is now the judgement nothing supersedes, ranked by chain length then decided_at/received_at/qualification_id so the order is total, with the recursive walk depth-capped at 64 because producer-supplied ids make a cycle reachable. Definition written into C-006 §1.4 so contract and code agree. Three db-specs were written first and observed failing against the old query; a fourth witnesses the depth cap.

s6d.status: DEPLOYED AND VERIFIED IN PRODUCTION 2026-07-23. Migration 007 applied via the migrate init container ('applied 007_touchpoint_and_campaign.sql, 1 migration(s) applied'). Tag 350a2ba.
s6d.what: Per-experiment and per-campaign measurement (F-007). attribution.touchpoint + the growth.touchpoints consumer; a lead's experiment derived at read time from lead.correlation_id -> auth_redirect.session_id -> touchpoint; spend event v2 with an optional campaignId; report gains spend.byCampaign and outOfScope {otherExperiments, noTouchpoint}; growth-web refuses to start without GROWTH_EXPERIMENT_ID/VERSION instead of defaulting to the literal 'unknown'.
s6d.ownerDecisions2026_07_23: Both gaps closed now rather than twice. Spend already recorded cannot be split afterwards, and the touchpoints carrying experimentId were being swept after 30 days by the ingest buffer retention.
s6d.derivedNotStored: experiment_id is NOT a column on qualification.lead. The touchpoint, the click and the lead arrive on three queues at three rates, so a value resolved at write time could never be recomputed. The read model resolves the view that led to the click (at or before initiated_at; nearest later view only when nothing precedes it, which is clock skew rather than a later visit).
s6d.tests: growth-core 308 (was 276), 24 suites; growth-web 84. Both typecheck clean. New: touchpoint storage + idempotency + runtime-role UPDATE/DELETE refusal, six lead-to-experiment derivation cases, campaign split/ordering/mixed-currency cases, v2 schema cases, landing fail-loud cases.
s6d.notDeployed: growth-core rollout (also carries the C-006 1.4 supersession fix), migration 007 via the migrate init container, growth-web rollout. See TASKS.md.

verifiedInProduction.migration: 007 applied by the init container as the owner role. Both DDL changes are proven by the live endpoint rather than by the log alone: GET /experiments/exp-001/report returned 200, and that response requires the LATERAL join over attribution.touchpoint AND the campaign_id column in spend.manual_observation to exist and be readable by the runtime role.
verifiedInProduction.queue: growth.touchpoints exists, bound to growth.events on growth.touchpoint.observed.v1, 1 consumer, 0 messages. Declared and bound by the consumer on boot, before any producer traffic depended on it.
verifiedInProduction.reportShape: Live response now carries spend.byCampaign and outOfScope. On production data it reads registrations 0 and outOfScope.noTouchpoint 4 — the four leads in the workspace have no stored touchpoint, so they are no longer counted against exp-001's spend. Before S6d the same four were reported as this experiment's registrations.
verifiedInProduction.landing: growth-web rolled out and serving (https://bazos.alfares.cz/l/v1-cena → 200). It now refuses to construct without GROWTH_EXPERIMENT_ID/VERSION; the ConfigMap supplies exp-001/v1, so the pod started clean.
verifiedInProduction.alsoShipped: The C-006 §1.4 supersession read-model fix, which had been written and tested but never rolled out.

s6d.knownConsequence: The four pre-existing leads can never be attributed to an experiment: no touchpoint was ever stored for them and the ingest buffer's 30-day retention has nothing to recover. They stay in outOfScope.noTouchpoint permanently, which is the truthful answer (F-007 'Explicitly excluded').
